description: A vertical, portrait-orientation testimonial/quote graphic produced by the U.S. Department of Labor. The background is a candid color photograph of two people standing close together and smiling at each other, framed from below against the steel girders of an industrial bridge or dock structure under a hazy sky. On the left is a woman with long dark brown hair wearing a light blue blazer; on the right is an older weathered white man with grey hair and a grey goatee wearing a light blue collared work shirt bearing a small red embroidered logo/patch on the chest. The lower two-thirds of the image fades into a deep navy-blue gradient overlay that makes the white text legible. A small circular medallion with stylized opening quotation marks ('66) sits centered above the quote. The quote, in white italic serif type, reads: 'Thanks to President Trump, the Gulf of America is going to survive. His policies have our' followed by the words 'COMPLETE SUPPORT!' set in very large bold white serif all-caps for emphasis. At the bottom, attribution in white reads 'ACY' (bold caps) over 'Louisiana Shrimper' (italic). Faint government seal watermarks are visible in the dark lower background. The messaging technique is the testimonial/plain-folks appeal: an ordinary blue-collar worker (a Gulf shrimper) is quoted endorsing President Trump's economic policies and validating the administration's branding of the Gulf of Mexico as the 'Gulf of America.' Target audience is working-class Americans and Trump supporters, intended to portray the administration's deregulation/America First agenda as directly helping everyday industry workers.
notable_text: 'Thanks to President Trump, the Gulf of America is going to survive. His policies have our COMPLETE SUPPORT!' - ACY, Louisiana Shrimper
